nl: If and only if Karen binge-watches a Netflix show, she will download it.
If Karen binge-watches a Netflix show, she will share it with Lisa.
translation_b: (exists x.(NetflixShow(x) & BingeWatch(karen)) -> exists x.(NetflixShow(x) & ShareWith(karen, lisa)))

nl: All highly intelligent beings from Earth are humans.
translation_b: all x.((HighlyIntelligent(x) & Being(x) & From(x, earth)) -> Human(x))

nl: If a person at the mixer is a celebrity, then they are well paid.
translation_b: all x.((Person(x) & At(x, theMixer) & Mixer(theMixer)) -> (Celebrity(x) -> WellPaid(x)))

nl: All well-paid people at the mixer live in tax havens.
translation_a: all x.((WellPaid(x) & Person(x) & At(x, theMixer)) -> exists y.(TaxHaven(y) & LiveIn(x, y)))

nl: The Working title "The Secret Dude Society" was used for Badults.
There are no mountain ranges in texas that have mines that have been donated.
translation_a: all x.(exists y.(exists z.((MountainRange(x) & In(x, texas) & Has(x, y) & Mine(y) & Has(y, z) & Donated(z)))) -> -MountainRange(x))

nl: The state-of-the-art text summarization model is trained with machine learning algorithms.
translation_b: exists x.(StateOfTheArtTextSummarizationModel(x) & exists y.(MachineLearningAlgorithm(y) & TrainedWith(x, y)))

nl: Everyone who can register to vote in the United States can participate in the 2024 United States presidential election.
translation_b: all x.((CanRegisterToVoteIn(x, unitedStates) & UnitedStates(unitedStates)) -> CanParticipateIn(x, presidentialElection2024))

nl: The winner of the 1992 du Maurier Classic was Steinhauer.
If a film contains excessive erotic and violent content, children cannot watch it without guidance from their parents.
translation_a: ∀x (Film(x) ∧ Contain(x, excessiveEroticContent) ∧ Contain(x, excessiveViolentContent) → ¬CanWatchWithout(children, x, guidanceFromParents))
translation_b: (exists x.((Film(x) & ContainsExcessiveEroticContent(x) & ContainsViolentContent(x)) & Film(x)) -> all y.(Child(y) -> exists z.(Parent(z) & -WatchWithoutGuidance(y, z))))

nl: If the stock price is bought to earn profits from rapid price appreciation, then it is not suitable for a retirement fund.
ETS provides financial aid to those GRE applicants who prove economic hardship.
translation_a: ∀x (ApplicantOf(x, gre) ∧ Prove(x, economicHardship) → ProvideTo(ets, financialAid, x))

nl: Reinforcement learning is not used to train the state-of-the-art text summarization model.
Imperium has a feud with Diamond Mine.
If a license plate begins with the number 35, then it does not begin with the number 34.
The city of St Pierre is not in the state of Montana.
All clumsy foodies who go out frequently to find new food restaurants to try are people who spill a lot of food on their clothing.
translation_a: all x.(exists y.((ClumsyFoodie(x) & GoOutFrequently(x) & Find(x, y) & NewFoodRestaurant(y))) -> (Person(x) & SpillFoodOnClothing(x)))

nl: Breeding back is a form of artificial selection by the deliberate selective breeding of domestic animals.
translation_a: exists x.((FormOf(x, y) & ArtificialSelection(y)) & exists z.(DomesticAnimal(z) & DeliberateSelectiveBreeding(x, z)))

nl: All games on the Top 10 list are made by Japanese game companies.
translation_b: all x.((Game(x) & OnTop10List(x)) -> exists y.(JapaneseGameCompany(y) & MadeBy(x, y)))

nl: Barutin Cove is on the southwest coast of Snow Island.
Anyone who has a well-attended wedding invites others to come to their ceremony.
translation_a: all x.(HasWellAttendedWedding(x) -> exists y.(Other(y) & InviteToCeremony(x, y)))
translation_b: ∀x (Have(x, wellAttendedWedding) → ∃y ∃z (¬(y=x) ∧ ¬(z=x) ∧ ¬(y=z) ∧ InviteToComeTo(x, y, wedding) ∧ InviteToComeTo(x, z, wedding)))

nl: Some people that have well-attended weddings have larger families.
translation_b: exists x.(exists y.((Person(x) & HaveWedding(x, y) & WellAttended(y))) & HaveLargerFamily(x))

nl: If a film is appropriate for people of all ages, then children can watch it without guidance from parents.
translation_b: (AppropriateForAllAges(film) -> all x.(Child(x) -> exists y.(Parent(y) & WatchWithoutGuidance(x, film))))

nl: Ted is an alligator.
If George likes music, he wants to compose.
If people spend a lot of time hanging out with and playing with their siblings, then they often live at home.
translation_b: (all x.(Person(x) -> exists y.(Sibling(y, x) & (SpendTimeWith(x, y) & PlayWith(x, y)))) -> all x.(Person(x) -> LiveAt(x, home)))

nl: No chemical compounds contain only one element.
All professional basketball players can do jump shots.
The last summer Olympic games was in Tokyo.
All mala hotpots are spicy.
Cats are pets.
Yale University moved to New Haven in 1716.
If Marvin is not from Earth, then Marvin is an extraterrestrial.
Tom is neither a Merriam's wild turkey nor a Rio Grande wild turkey.
Space is a vacuum.
The Surprising Adventures of Sir Digby Chicken Caesar is a superhero movie.
Karen does not download "Black Mirror."
Humans are mammals.
All humans are mortal.
